title: Two Dozen And One Greyhounds

jokes.text: In today's news, a 2-ton rhino escaped from the Springfield Zoo. But zoo officials were quick to act...and Petunia, as she is known, is safely back in captivity. In other news, a 3-ton rhino...that escaped from the zoo last week is still at large.
jokes.setup: News report about zoo officials being 'quick to act' on escaped rhino
jokes.punchline: Reveal that there's another, bigger rhino that's been loose for a week
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons absurdist news gag with perfect misdirection structure - sets up competence then immediately undercuts it

jokes.text: Who spread garbage all over Flanders' yard before I got a chance to?
jokes.setup: Homer listing damaged/destroyed items in angry questioning tone
jokes.punchline: Reveals his petty motivation about the Flanders prank
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character comedy - starts serious then reveals petty selfishness. Great misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Oh, please. This is senseless destruction...with none of my usual social commentary.
jokes.setup: Homer examining destructive behavior
jokes.punchline: Claims his destruction usually has social commentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke that gives Homer unearned intellectual pretension while staying perfectly in character

jokes.text: He's lucky too...because it's spanking season, and I got a hankering for some spankering.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring Bart lucky not to be blamed
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'spankering' combining spanking with hankering
jokes.explanation: Decent Homer wordplay with his characteristic verbal bumbling, though not exceptional

jokes.text: My Best of Ray Stevens, featuring 'The Streak,' album.
jokes.setup: Listing buried possessions
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and embarrassing musical taste
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that reveals Homer's questionable taste in a specific, believable way

jokes.text: Oh, my God! He's got the precious cable-TV cable!
jokes.setup: Homer surveying destroyed items
jokes.punchline: Cable TV is most important thing to him
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Homer's priorities, predictable but effective

jokes.text: Your cable TV is experiencing difficulties. Please do not panic. Resist the temptation to read or talk to loved ones. Do not attempt sexual relations...as years of TV radiation have left your genitals withered and useless.
jokes.setup: Standard cable outage message
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd warnings about TV addiction and radiation damage
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark satire that starts mundane then escalates to disturbing absurdity with perfect deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Well, I'll be damned.
jokes.setup: Extreme TV warning about withered genitals
jokes.punchline: Homer's mild, impressed reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction beat - Homer's casual acceptance of absurd information is peak character comedy

jokes.text: I'll tell you what I'm telling everyone else. I'm sorry if your dog went blind, but your gripe is with Hartz Mountain, not me.
jokes.setup: Simpsons asking for help with their dog problem
jokes.punchline: Clerk assumes it's about dog blindness from flea products
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag that creates absurd world-building about common pet store problems

jokes.text: Let me try a canine-human mind meld. It's an incredibly rare psychic power possessed only by me...and three other clerks at this store.
jokes.setup: Clerk claiming rare psychic power
jokes.punchline: Deflation that three other minimum-wage clerks also have it
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that builds up mystic power then immediately deflates it with mundane reality

jokes.text: I'm bored. I'm restless. Need change in life...like imported leather leash...blue contact lenses...200-volt-shock training collar.
jokes.setup: Mind meld revealing dog's emotional needs
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable to expensive to dangerous products
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation gag that parodies pet store upselling, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Don't worry. We'll catch him or run him over trying.
jokes.setup: Homer reassuring about catching the escaped dog
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions potentially deadly alternative
jokes.explanation: Good dark Homer humor with efficient structure, though the format is familiar

jokes.text: Don't worry, when they find out he doesn't have money...they'll throw him out. Believe me, I know.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why dog will be kicked out of race track
jokes.punchline: Reveals this happened to him personally
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment that reveals Homer's gambling problems and personal experience with shame

jokes.text: Two bucks to win on number eight.
jokes.setup: Marge asking if they should split up to look for dog
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately starts gambling instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priority fail - perfectly in character but predictable

jokes.text: You're lucky you got looks, Marge.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning Homer's gambling logic about dog name
jokes.punchline: Homer's backhanded compliment implying she's not smart
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer insensitivity disguised as compliment - effective character comedy but familiar territory

jokes.text: Yes! Come on, you little horse!
jokes.setup: Homer cheering on his bet at greyhound race
jokes.punchline: Calls greyhound a horse
jokes.explanation: Simple Homer ignorance gag - gets a chuckle but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Looks like he's trying to jump over her, but he can't quite make it.
jokes.setup: Announcer describing the dogs' interaction during the race
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of what's clearly the dogs mating
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemistic comedy - the announcer's innocent sports commentary describing obvious mating behavior.

jokes.text: So that's what's been wrong with the little fella. He misses casual sex.
jokes.setup: Realizing why dog was misbehaving
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude interpretation in front of children
jokes.explanation: Good contrast with Lisa's innocent comment - Homer's inappropriate directness creates discomfort

jokes.text: Won't you miss her loyalty and companionship?
jokes.setup: Marge asking track owner about giving up the female greyhound
jokes.punchline: Sets up the owner's cold response
jokes.explanation: Standard setup line - works to contrast with owner's business attitude but not funny alone

jokes.text: Lady, you're all right.
jokes.setup: Marge's sentimental question about loyalty and companionship
jokes.punchline: Cold, business-like dismissal of emotional attachment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing callous business attitude - economical and effective

jokes.text: Me and Santa's Little Helper used to be a team. But he never wants to play anymore since his bitch moved in.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining about dog ignoring him
jokes.punchline: Uses technically correct but inappropriate word for female dog
jokes.explanation: Classic kid-says-bad-word gag with technical correctness defense - effective discomfort comedy

jokes.text: Well, I'm going to write the dictionary people and have that checked. Feels like a mistake to me.
jokes.setup: Bart using correct but offensive terminology
jokes.punchline: Marge thinks dictionary is wrong rather than accept the word
jokes.explanation: Excellent Marge character comedy - her denial of reality to maintain propriety is perfectly absurd and quotable

jokes.text: Hey, look. A really small dog just fell out of Santa's girlfriend.
jokes.setup: Family discovering the birth
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact description of puppy birth
jokes.explanation: Good child's perspective on birth - innocent but funny description of natural process

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry, girl. I thought you were just getting fat.
jokes.setup: Realizing dog was pregnant, not gaining weight
jokes.punchline: Homer's tactless apology about calling her fat
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer foot-in-mouth moment - insensitive but believable character beat

jokes.text: Twenty-five.
jokes.setup: Counting puppies up to 24
jokes.punchline: Final count reveals absurdly large litter
jokes.explanation: Good escalation payoff - builds to absurd number that drives the episode plot

jokes.text: An army of dogs! No bully will ever touch me again.
jokes.setup: Seeing 25 puppies
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately thinks of using them as protection
jokes.explanation: Good Bart character moment - immediately sees opportunity for mischief and protection

jokes.text: Hey, jerkface. You have the face of a jerk.
jokes.setup: Bart testing his new confidence with dog army
jokes.punchline: Redundant insult that explains the obvious
jokes.explanation: Kid-logic insult that's funny for its circular reasoning, though not sophisticated

jokes.text: Say, Jimbo...hope I wasn't out of line with that 'jerkface' crack.
jokes.setup: Bart had just insulted bully with dog protection
jokes.punchline: False politeness after the dogs scared off the bully
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic reversal - Bart's fake politeness after gaining upper hand shows character growth

jokes.text: Yes. We waited many years...but the good Lord has finally blessed us with a real family.
jokes.setup: Marge calling puppies adorable
jokes.punchline: Homer implies his human family isn't 'real' compared to dogs
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark Homer moment that reveals his twisted priorities - insulting his human family while being sincere

jokes.text: Look at Branford II. Isn't that cute? He thinks he's one of the Models Inc.
jokes.setup: Homer naming and describing puppy behavior
jokes.punchline: Oddly sophisticated pop culture reference about 90s TV show
jokes.explanation: Unexpected cultural reference for Homer - funny contrast between his usual ignorance and specific TV knowledge

jokes.text: Mom, why do I have to wear a flea collar?
jokes.setup: House overrun with puppies and fleas
jokes.punchline: Family members wearing flea collars like the dogs
jokes.explanation: Great visual escalation showing how far the dog situation has deteriorated - absurd but logical

jokes.text: Oh, it's just easier this way.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning why he needs to wear flea collar
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact acceptance of absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - practical acceptance of insane situation with resigned tone

jokes.text: Are you just putting new newspapers over the old ones?
jokes.setup: Homer supposedly cleaning up dog waste
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just layering newspapers instead of cleaning
jokes.explanation: Good Homer laziness gag - logical but gross solution that shows his character

jokes.text: Do you have a better idea?
jokes.setup: Marge discovering his lazy cleaning method
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely thinks this is reasonable solution
jokes.explanation: Solid Homer defense of lazy behavior - perfectly in character but predictable

jokes.text: Yeah, they ate all my socks. I have to wear Lisa's to school today.
jokes.setup: Family discussing dog problems
jokes.punchline: Bart forced to wear sister's clothing due to dogs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of dog chaos affecting daily life - embarrassing consequence for Bart

jokes.text: Is that what we do in this family? When someone becomes an inconvenience, we just get rid of them?
jokes.setup: Parents wanting to give away puppies
jokes.punchline: Bart's dramatic appeal about family loyalty
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Bart making moral argument about something he'll later want to do himself

jokes.text: Hello? Is anybody there?
jokes.setup: Bart's question about getting rid of inconvenient family members
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to abandoned Grandpa calling out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual punchline with perfect timing - immediately answers Bart's question with dark humor

jokes.text: On a completely unrelated topic...I'm having a very, very important dinner party tonight.
jokes.setup: Just agreed to keep the dogs
jokes.punchline: Obviously related dinner party that will test this decision
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom ironic setup - obviously setting up disaster with false casual tone

jokes.text: Reverend Lovejoy, your old Army drill sergeant...and the regional director of the IRS.
jokes.setup: Important dinner party
jokes.punchline: Three worst possible guests for chaos to happen around
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of stakes - three authority figures who would judge Homer harshly

jokes.text: The whole town will hear what a perfect evening this was. How nothing at all went wrong.
jokes.setup: Dinner party with important guests
jokes.punchline: Homer's overconfident prediction guaranteeing disaster
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony setup - Homer's overconfidence predicting his downfall

jokes.text: And to the delectable turkey...that's walking around the table!
jokes.setup: Formal dinner toast
jokes.punchline: Turkey being chased by puppies around formal table
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy payoff - formal toast interrupted by chaos, perfect timing and escalation

jokes.text: Dear Lord, it's a demon bird.
jokes.setup: Chaos at dinner table
jokes.punchline: Religious authority calling turkey demonic
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reaction - reverend's religious interpretation of mundane chaos

jokes.text: See you in hell. From heaven.
jokes.setup: Angry departure from ruined dinner
jokes.punchline: Reverend's religious twist on standard threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific joke - perfect religious authority version of angry departure threat

jokes.text: We could use a new pet over at the home...seeing how we accidentally killed that smart-mouthed bird.
jokes.setup: Offering to take some puppies
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of accidentally killing previous pet
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - casual mention of pet death with specific detail about 'smart-mouthed bird'

jokes.text: And I know the little fellas would love romping around my many acres...chasing my many cars...drinking from my many toilets.
jokes.setup: Burns describing how he'd care for puppies
jokes.punchline: Escalating from nice to weird to gross activities
jokes.explanation: Good Burns character moment - starts appealing then reveals his weird rich person perspective

jokes.text: She's right, Homer. There's something about his face I don't trust.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning about Burns' intentions
jokes.punchline: Marge's shallow but accurate judgment
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment - Marge's intuitive distrust based on superficial observation

jokes.text: A little too well, if you ask me.
jokes.setup: Homer noting Burns took rejection well
jokes.punchline: Marge's suspicious observation setting up his scheme
jokes.explanation: Standard suspicious reaction line - functional but predictable

jokes.text: Honestly, sir, you don't put the effort into your schemes that you used to.
jokes.setup: Burns using simple kidnapping instead of elaborate plan
jokes.punchline: Smithers critiquing the quality of Burns' evil plots
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about villain tropes while staying in character - Smithers as disappointed evil assistant

jokes.text: No, I'm not. No, I'm not. I'm dusting for prints.
jokes.setup: Accused of getting powdered sugar everywhere
jokes.punchline: Claims he's dusting for fingerprints with donut powder
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum incompetence gag - turns his donut habit into fake police work

jokes.text: You know who it reminds me of?
jokes.setup: Burns finding puppy adorable
jokes.punchline: Sets up the bizarre Rory Calhoun references
jokes.explanation: Setup for upcoming Rory Calhoun gag - not funny alone but necessary

jokes.text: Some men hunt for sport, Others hunt for food, The only thing I'm hunting for, Is an outfit that looks good
jokes.setup: Musical number beginning
jokes.punchline: Parody of hunting songs but for making clothes from animals
jokes.explanation: Excellent musical comedy setup - parodies noble hunting songs for vain, evil purpose

jokes.text: Some men hunt for sport, Others hunt for food, The only thing I'm hunting for Is an outfit that looks good
jokes.setup: Musical number about hunting
jokes.punchline: Reveals shallow fashion motivation instead of traditional hunting reasons
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the entire musical sequence. Subverts hunting song conventions for fashion vanity. Well-crafted lyrics with good rhythm.

jokes.text: See my vest, See my vest, Made from real gorilla chest
jokes.setup: Musical number about clothing
jokes.punchline: Vest made from endangered animal with rhyming structure
jokes.explanation: Iconic musical moment - perfectly crafted villain song with memorable rhyme scheme and dark humor

jokes.text: These white slippers are albino African endangered rhino
jokes.setup: Continuing clothing catalog song
jokes.punchline: Forced rhyme about endangered species footwear
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of vest song with complex rhyme scheme and escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Like my loafers? Former gophers. It was that or skin my chauffeurs
jokes.setup: More clothing items in song
jokes.punchline: Rhymes gophers with chauffeurs while threatening employees
jokes.explanation: Peak villain song moment - clever rhyming with casual threat to employees, perfect Burns character

jokes.text: I really like the vest.
jokes.setup: Elaborate musical number about evil clothing
jokes.punchline: Smithers' simple, supportive response to elaborate evil song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers reaction - understated approval of boss's elaborate evil plan shows their relationship

jokes.text: Sorry. You gotta admit, it's catchy.
jokes.setup: Family horrified by Burns' evil plan
jokes.punchline: Bart admitting the villain song was well-crafted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment where character acknowledges the quality of the musical number they just witnessed

jokes.text: No. What are you doing? Dogs always land on their feet.
jokes.setup: Trying to escape through window with puppies
jokes.punchline: Bart confuses cats and dogs for basic animal facts
jokes.explanation: Good kid logic failure - Bart's dangerous confusion of basic animal facts

jokes.text: Best two out of three.
jokes.setup: Wrong about dogs landing on their feet
jokes.punchline: Wants to continue dangerous experiment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Bart's reckless scientific method - doubles down on dangerous approach

jokes.text: That's impossible. How did you get here first?
jokes.setup: Burns catches them at bottom of laundry chute
jokes.punchline: Classic cartoon villain appearing where he shouldn't be able to
jokes.explanation: Standard cartoon physics gag - villain impossibly appearing ahead of protagonists

jokes.text: Here's a phone. Call somebody who cares.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about theft
jokes.punchline: Burns' dismissive response with prop comedy
jokes.explanation: Classic villain dismissal line with visual prop - works for Burns' character but not original

jokes.text: This can't be happening. They're all standing.
jokes.setup: Burns trying to identify his favorite puppy by standing trick
jokes.punchline: All puppies learned the trick, foiling his plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation payoff - Burns' plan defeated by puppies' natural learning ability

jokes.text: But they're so wretchedly adorable. Twenty-five little Rory Calhouns.
jokes.setup: Burns about to kill all the puppies
jokes.punchline: Returns to obscure actor reference while being overwhelmed by cuteness
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Burns' character consistency while being overwhelmed by emotion

jokes.text: Look at you, standing there on your hind legs...like a couple of Rory Calhouns.
jokes.setup: Burns threatening the children
jokes.punchline: Another Rory Calhoun reference about the kids standing
jokes.explanation: Good running gag continuation - Burns applies his odd reference to human children now

jokes.text: I swear, I'll never wear any clothing made from an animal...that can do an amusing trick.
jokes.setup: Burns seeming to reform about animal cruelty
jokes.punchline: Very specific loophole that misses the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Burns 'learns' lesson but in most selfish way possible, perfect moral loophole

jokes.text: I'll bet whoever gave him those dogs is kicking themselves now.
jokes.setup: News about Burns' racing success with the dogs
jokes.punchline: Direct reference to Homer's decision, setting up his reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic setup line - functional for Homer's reaction but predictable

jokes.text: Marge, you know that batting this light bulb is the only thing...that cheers me up after giving away those million-dollar greyhounds.
jokes.setup: Homer dealing with regret over losing valuable dogs
jokes.punchline: Coping by acting like a cat with a light bulb
jokes.explanation: Excellent ending gag - Homer's bizarre coping mechanism perfectly captures his childlike regression

tmdb_overview: The Simpson household's overrun with puppies--the offspring of Santa's Little Helper and his new girlfriend from which Mr. Burns would love to make a greyhound-skin tuxedo.
insight: Racing plot escalates to 67 jokes in 22 minutes—a dense comedy engine built on character absurdity.
